title: Architecture Variants (Encoder-Only, Decoder-Only, Encoder-Decoder)
summary: Three transformer variants. Encoder-only (BERT): bidirectional, understanding. Decoder-only (GPT): causal, generation. Encoder-decoder (T5): sequence-to-sequence. Selection depends on task structure.
key_insights: Encoder-only: bidirectional, no generation. Classification, retrieval, similarity, anomaly detection, knowledge storage
Decoder-only: causal, generation by continuation. Conversation and story completion are architecturally identical
Encoder-decoder: distinct input→output transformation. Translation, summarization
Decision tree: (1) Generate? No → encoder-only. (2) Distinct I/O? Yes → enc-dec. (3) Same stream → dec-only
Encoder-only = best for knowledge storage/retrieval (richest bidirectional representation)

mathematical_core.encoder_only: Output = f_classify(Encoder(X)) or Output = Encoder(X) as embedding
mathematical_core.decoder_only: P(x_t | x_{<t}) = softmax(Decoder(x_{<t})) — next token prediction, causal mask always applied
mathematical_core.encoder_decoder: P(y_t | y_{<t}, X) = softmax(Decoder(y_{<t}, Encoder(X))) — conditioned on encoded input

derivations.title: Bidirectional vs causal — information-theoretic perspective

steps.name: Encoder (bidirectional)
steps.math: Token i's representation h_i = f(x_1, ..., x_n) — conditioned on ALL tokens. Maximum mutual information I(h_i; X) with the full input.

steps.name: Decoder (causal)
steps.math: Token i's representation h_i = f(x_1, ..., x_i) — conditioned only on past. I(h_i; X) ≤ I(h_i; x_1..x_i) < I(h_i; x_1..x_n). Strictly less information.

steps.name: Consequence
steps.math: For understanding tasks (where you need maximum info about the input), encoder-only is strictly better. For generation tasks, the causal constraint is necessary (can't condition on future tokens you haven't generated yet).

derivations.consequence: The bidirectional/causal choice is not a quality tradeoff — it's a constraint imposed by the task. Understanding tasks allow bidirectional. Generation tasks require causal.

derivations.title: Parameter efficiency comparison
derivations.math: For same total budget P: encoder-only uses all P for understanding. Decoder-only uses all P for generation. Encoder-decoder splits: ~40% encoder + ~53% decoder + ~7% embeddings. The enc-dec model has less capacity per function but covers both comprehension and generation.
derivations.consequence: For tasks that need ONLY understanding or ONLY generation, a single-stack model uses parameters more efficiently than encoder-decoder.

related: FOUNDATIONS.INDUCTIVE_BIAS — variant selection = choosing inductive bias for task
